PART I
3 SPFLLS

data.components: V, S, M (a pinch of dust from another plane and a small mirror) or V, S, M (special glasses, a metal rod, a crystal vial, and an exotic incantation) depending on the type of mirage desired
data.description: You create an illusory duplicate that resembles you in appearance and size. The duplicate can take actions and move on its own or be controlled by another creature who can see it. If a creature successfully targets the duplicate with a spell, the spell targets you instead. If the duplicate is targeted with a harmful spell or effect and takes any damage, you suffer the same damage.

components: M (a vial of poisoned blood, which the spell consumes)
data.description: You unleash a blast of withering energy in a 20-foot cube centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature in that area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 4d8 necrotic damage and has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the start of your next turn.

data.components: V, S, M (a bowl and spoon worth at least 100 gp each)
V, S, M (an obsidian rod worth at least 50 gp, which the spell consumes)
data.description: You call down a bolt of lightning that strikes a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 8d6 thunder damage, or half as much on a successful one.

components: M (a glass vial and a small amount of mercury, which the spell consumes)
data.description: The target and all objects it is wearing and carrying are projected into the Astral Plane. They gain the ability to move about freely in that plane for the duration.
